text:
----Security_Multipart(Tue_Sep_13_12_13_27_2011_208)--
Content-Type: Text/Plain; charset=us-ascii
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Root User <leftfoot@siu.edu> wrote
  in <201109122209.p8CM9mfT016776@red.freebsd.org>:

le> Execute acroread (from command line):
le>
le> 1) acroread window comes up and shortly goes away
le> 2) following messages are displayed
le>
le> Gtk-Message: Failed to load module "canberra-gtk-module": libcanberra-gtk-module.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
le> Gtk-Message: Failed to load module "gnomesegvhandler": libgnomesegvhandler.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory

 I think these have nothing to do with the acroread9 crash.

le> terminate called after throwing an instance of 'RSException'

 Did you do the following (this should be displayed after the
 installation)?

----
Before using Adobe Reader 9, linux_adobe.ko kernel module has to be loaded.
It is automatically loaded by /usr/local/etc/rc.d/linux_adobe script on
boot time.  You can manually load it by

 # /usr/local/etc/rc.d/linux_adobe start

without rebooting.
----

-- Hiroki
